MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rahman Popal, Esq. of The Law Firm for Tenant Rights for Plaintiff, Martha Orozco via Zoom VideoConference.
Vanessa Dao, Esq. of Schumann Arevalo LLP for Defendants, King Xiong Chong and Maggie Ho via Zoom VideoConference.

NOT ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS KING CHONG, MAGGIE HO's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently states facts to support the allegation of reckless disregard for Plaintiff's right. Plaintiff provided authorities regarding prayer for prejudgment interest in opposition. Defendant does not address its argument or Plaintiff's authorities in reply.

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: S. Le; Proceedings not reported. (501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rahman Popal, Esq. of The Law Firm for Tenant Rights for Plaintiff, Martha Orozco via Zoom VideoConference.
Vanessa Dao, Esq. of Schumann Arevalo LLP for Defendants, King Xiong Chong and Maggie Ho via Zoom VideoConference.

NOT ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KING CHONG, MAGGIE HO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.
Demurer to the cause of action for trespass is sustained with leave to amend on uncertainty grounds for Plaintiff to allege what acts of the defendants constitute trespass and the timeframe of the trespass. It is unclear from the complaint if P alleges that Defendants have been trespassing since the date of fire or since some later date; whether Plaintiff claims that defendants commit trespass by withholding possession; by entering for remodeling or by some other acts. The Court does not reach the issue of the sufficiency of allegations at this time. Demurrer to the cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend on failure to state facts and uncertainty grounds. It is unclear whether Plaintiff assets fraud based on misrepresentation or fraud based on concealment or both. To the extent Plaintiff attempts to allege fraud based on misrepresentation, the elements are not alleged with requisite particularity. To the extent Plaintiff attempts to allege fraud based on concealment, no facts are alleged to show that Plaintiff "would not have acted as [s]he did if [a]he had known of the concealed or suppressed fact."Demurer is otherwise overruled. All other causes of action subject to this demurrer are sufficiently stated and are not uncertain. Declaration of Plaintiff's counsel is stricken as not appropriate on a demurrer.

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: S. Le; Proceedings not reported. (501)
